description_long: This is a short section of a wooden picket fence, featuring five vertical slats with pointed tops and a single diagonal rail connecting them. The slats are unevenly spaced and slightly irregular in shape, giving the fence a rustic, weathered appearance. The diagonal rail is fastened to the slats with visible round metal bolts. The wood is painted a dark, almost black color with subtle hints of blue and brown, suggesting exposure to the elements or intentional staining. The overall construction is simple, with no decorative embellishments or ornate features. This type of fence is commonly found in residential yards, gardens, or parks to demarcate boundaries or provide a modest barrier.
description: A rustic, dark-painted wooden picket fence section with five pointed slats and a single diagonal rail fastened by metal bolts.
description_view_0: Front view showing five pointed slats, a diagonal rail with visible bolts, and the irregular spacing of the pickets.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the flatness of the slats and rail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same five slats and diagonal rail, with the bolts less prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the alignment of the slats and rail.
